what_it_does: Implements an HTTP speech-to-text ear on port 2032 that wraps NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B through the NeMo toolkit. It accepts multipart audio via POST /inference, transcribes it using either the SALM (speech-augmented LLM) path or a generic NeMo ASRModel fallback, and exposes a liveness GET / endpoint through a shared _stt_service wrapper.
observations: Top-level docstring documents the model card API, install command, and a CUDA-13 hazard.
Uses environment variables COMPANY_CANARY_MODEL and COMPANY_CANARY_DEVICE.
Lazy singleton _engine loads the model once and caches kind ('salm' or 'asr').
Explicitly moves the model to DEVICE when cuda is requested because NeMo loads to CPU by default.
Sets the model to eval mode after loading.
transcribe writes incoming bytes to a temporary .wav and dispatches based on _kind.
SALM path uses model.audio_locator_tag in a user prompt and tokenizer decoding.
Generic ASR path calls model.transcribe and extracts text from the first hypothesis.
standouts: Explicit .to(DEVICE) to prevent silent CPU slow-path degradation in the voice loop.
SALM/LLM-ASR generate path with audio-locator prompt.
Fail-loud RuntimeError with install guidance when NeMo/Canary support is missing.
Docstring warns about ~6-7 GB fp16 VRAM and co-residency with the chat brain.
conventions: Temporary file for audio bytes before model ingestion.
sys.path manipulation for relative package import.
Extensive docstring with verification date and API snippet.
concerns: Temporary file uses delete=True while still open and referenced by the model; may behave differently across platforms.
SALM prompt and max_new_tokens differ slightly between docstring and implementation.
Generic ASR fallback assumes hyps[0] shape and extracts text defensively but without detailed error handling.
Only ImportError is caught in the fallback; other load-time failures propagate as exceptions.
notes: A fail-loud, GPU-explicit HTTP adapter that brings NVIDIA Canary-Qwen into the voice-loop as an STT ear, with a guarded SALM-first loading strategy.
questions: What contract does voice.ears._stt_service.serve enforce for warm and transcribe?
Is the incoming audio always a valid WAV, or should it be validated/converted?
Is model.audio_locator_tag guaranteed to exist on every SALM build used here?
How is the CUDA-13 hazard detected and surfaced beyond the RuntimeError?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation: it is an HTTP STT ear for Canary-Qwen with SALM/ASR fallback and explicit device placement.
apparent_themes: Explicit resource placement over silent degradation
intention_signals: Prevent the voice loop from silently falling back to CPU transcription; provide a local HTTP endpoint for a heavy LLM-based ASR model while keeping it isolated in its own NeMo environment.
novelty: Uses a speech-augmented LLM (SALM) prompt with an audio locator tag for ASR, and treats silent CPU placement as a hazard to be actively avoided.
contribution: A device-explicit, dependency-guarded HTTP adapter that exposes NVIDIA Canary-Qwen transcription as a local STT service with SALM-first and generic-ASR fallback paths.
summary_for_embedding: voice/ears/canary.py is an HTTP STT service wrapper around NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B using NeMo SALM/ASR, exposing POST /inference audio-to-text on port 2032 with explicit GPU placement and loud failure on missing dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a local voice-loop ear that runs NVIDIA Canary-Qwen as an HTTP STT endpoint while preventing silent CPU fallback.

symbols.does: Lazy-loads the Canary-Qwen model via SALM or a generic NeMo ASRModel and places it on the configured DEVICE.
symbols.description: _engine attempts to import SALM.from_pretrained first; if that fails with ImportError it falls back to nemo_asr.models.ASRModel.from_pretrained. When DEVICE starts with 'cuda' it explicitly moves the model to the GPU, sets eval mode, and caches both the model and the detected kind ('salm' or 'asr').

symbols.does: Writes incoming audio bytes to a temporary WAV file and returns a transcription dict using the loaded model.
symbols.description: transcribe obtains the cached model and kind from _engine, persists the raw audio bytes to a temporary .wav, then either calls SALM generate with an audio-locator user prompt and decodes the output with the tokenizer, or calls the generic ASR transcribe method and extracts text from the first hypothesis. It returns {"text": text}.
